inflection_resolutions.topic: Rules variant the encoded basic-strategy table targets
inflection_resolutions.rationale: Quick-reasoning rubric all four conditions hold: (1) all three rules variants are well-known canonical answers in training; (2) implementation complexity is similar across branches (H17 + late surrender adds only two cells of action-decision logic); (3) no new external dependencies introduced regardless of branch; (4) easily reversible by amending the strategy table and dealer auto-play function. ProfitDuel-style cheat sheets most commonly assume S17 + DAS + no-surrender, so this default minimizes mismatch with the user's referenced image. Locking to default branch.

machine_checkable_assertions.statement: Dealer stands on all 17 (S17), including soft 17
machine_checkable_assertions.scenario: dealer hand totals 17 (hard or soft) at decision point
machine_checkable_assertions.expected_result: playDealer returns immediately; no further card drawn; final dealer total equals 17

machine_checkable_assertions.scenario: after a split, on the first decision of either split sub-hand with two cards dealt
machine_checkable_assertions.expected_result: 'double' is included in returned actions array

machine_checkable_assertions.statement: Surrender is not a legal action in any state
machine_checkable_assertions.scenario: any player decision point throughout a hand
machine_checkable_assertions.expected_result: 'surrender' never appears in returned actions array

machine_checkable_assertions.scenario: player holds natural blackjack (A + ten-value) and dealer does not, with bet=10
machine_checkable_assertions.expected_result: player payout returned as 15 chips on top of the original bet (1.5x)

inflection_resolutions.topic: Mode structure: free play vs drill mode vs both
inflection_resolutions.chosen_branch: Free play only (deal real hands, hint on demand, post-hand review)
inflection_resolutions.rationale: Quick-reasoning rubric all four conditions hold: (1) free-play and drill-mode are well-known training UI patterns; (2) drill mode adds a scenario generator and separate UI surface, but the prompt's wording 'during a hand' / 'after a hand' explicitly implies hand-by-hand play, so the default is what the prompt asks for; (3) no new dependencies; (4) drill mode can be added later without breaking free play. Locking to default.

machine_checkable_assertions.statement: Application has no drill-mode UI surface or mode-selector
machine_checkable_assertions.expected_result: no element with id/class matching 'drill', 'mode-select', or 'mode-toggle'

machine_checkable_assertions.statement: App enters a free-play loop on load: betting -> dealing -> player decisions -> dealer play -> resolution -> review -> next
machine_checkable_assertions.scenario: after init() with no further action
machine_checkable_assertions.expected_result: phase begins at 'betting' or 'dealable' (not at any drill scenario)

inflection_resolutions.chosen_branch: Per-decision review (each action vs correct action)
inflection_resolutions.rationale: Quick-reasoning rubric all four conditions hold: (1) per-decision review is the canonical training pattern; (2) implementation cost difference is small once the engine logs every decision; (3) no new dependencies; (4) trivially reversible by collapsing the review into a verdict. Per-decision is meaningfully more educational and is what the prompt's 'suggestions after a hand' implies most usefully. Locking to default.

machine_checkable_assertions.statement: Each player decision (hit/stand/double/split) is appended to a per-hand decision log with the player hand snapshot and dealer upcard at decision time
machine_checkable_assertions.expected_pattern: after a resolved hand, getDecisionLog() returns an Array where each entry contains { playerHandSnapshot, dealerUpcard, chosenAction, correctAction }

machine_checkable_assertions.statement: Post-hand review panel lists every decision the user made, not just an aggregate verdict
machine_checkable_assertions.scenario: after a hand resolves with N>=2 player decisions
machine_checkable_assertions.expected_result: review panel renders N rows, one per logged decision

inflection_resolutions.rationale: Quick-reasoning rubric all four conditions hold: (1) showing a single action label is the canonical minimum hint UI; (2) reasoning text or chart-cell highlight materially increases scope and copy work; (3) no new dependencies needed for the minimal branch; (4) easily upgradable later by enriching the hint payload. Locking to default.

machine_checkable_assertions.statement: Hint output is a single action label (one of hit/stand/double/split), with no accompanying reasoning text or cheat-sheet visualization
machine_checkable_assertions.expected_pattern: the hint surface displays a string in the set {'Hit','Stand','Double','Split'} and no additional explanatory paragraph or chart cell

machine_checkable_assertions.statement: No cheat-sheet image or table is rendered as part of the hint
machine_checkable_assertions.scenario: while a hint is being shown
machine_checkable_assertions.expected_result: no , , or chart element appears within the hint container

inflection_resolutions.topic: Whether to display the cheat-sheet chart in-app
inflection_resolutions.chosen_branch: Do not embed the chart; trainer is the interactive surface
inflection_resolutions.rationale: Quick-reasoning rubric all four conditions hold: (1) embed-vs-don't-embed is a canonical product choice; (2) embedding adds image asset handling or chart-rendering code; (3) not embedding avoids the question of hotlinking the user's referenced image (no new external asset dependency); (4) trivially addable later. Locking to default.

machine_checkable_assertions.statement: No basic-strategy chart image, rendered chart table, or chart panel appears anywhere in the app DOM
machine_checkable_assertions.scenario: DOM rendered at any point in the hand lifecycle
machine_checkable_assertions.expected_result: no element with id/class matching 'chart', 'cheat-sheet', or 'strategy-chart'; no with src referencing a strategy chart

machine_checkable_assertions.statement: The strategy table is consumed only as a programmatic lookup (single source of truth for hint and review), not displayed
machine_checkable_assertions.expected_pattern: strategy-table exposes a function recommendation(player, dealerUpcard) but exposes no rendering function or template

sections.name: rules-engine
sections.charter: Pure JavaScript module implementing standard blackjack mechanics: shoe construction (multi-deck) with shuffling, hand scoring with proper ace handling (soft vs hard totals), blackjack and bust detection, legal-actions evaluator for the current hand state, dealer auto-play under S17, and hand resolution with 3:2 blackjack payout. Exposes pure functions only; no DOM, no storage, no global mutation. Single source of truth for all rules-of-the-game questions.
sections.acceptance: Given any sequence of card draws, the module returns correct hand totals, correctly identifies blackjacks and busts, returns the legal action set per the locked ruleset (hit/stand/double/split, with double-after-split allowed and surrender absent), plays the dealer to S17 standing, and resolves hands at 3:2 for blackjack. Module is unit-testable in isolation in a Node sandbox without DOM stubs.

acceptance_assertions.from_acceptance_phrase: returns correct hand totals
acceptance_assertions.scenario: scoreHand([A, 7]) -> soft 18; scoreHand([A, 7, K]) -> hard 18; scoreHand([10, 6, 5]) -> 21; scoreHand([10, 6, 7]) -> bust 23
acceptance_assertions.expected_result: totals match the values above; isSoft true only on the [A,7] case

acceptance_assertions.from_acceptance_phrase: correctly identifies blackjacks and busts
acceptance_assertions.scenario: scoreHand([A, K]) flagged as blackjack; scoreHand([10, 6, 6]) flagged as bust; scoreHand([A, K, 5]) NOT flagged as blackjack (3 cards)
acceptance_assertions.expected_result: isBlackjack and isBust flags are set per the rules above

acceptance_assertions.from_acceptance_phrase: returns the legal action set per the locked ruleset (hit/stand/double/split, with double-after-split allowed and surrender absent)
acceptance_assertions.scenario: legalActions on initial 2-card hand of [8,8] vs dealer 6 returns set including hit, stand, double, split; legalActions after one hit returns {hit, stand} only (no double, no split); legalActions on first decision of a split sub-hand with two cards returns set including double
acceptance_assertions.expected_result: returned arrays match the rule above; 'surrender' never appears

acceptance_assertions.from_acceptance_phrase: plays the dealer to S17 standing
acceptance_assertions.scenario: playDealer starting from [A,6] (soft 17): dealer stands and returns total 17. playDealer starting from [10,6]: dealer hits at least once.
acceptance_assertions.expected_result: S17 behavior verified for soft 17 and for any 17

acceptance_assertions.from_acceptance_phrase: resolves hands at 3:2 for blackjack
acceptance_assertions.scenario: resolveHand with player=[A,K], dealer=[10,9], bet=10
acceptance_assertions.expected_result: outcome 'player_blackjack'; player payout is 15 in addition to original bet returned

acceptance_assertions.from_acceptance_phrase: Module is unit-testable in isolation in a Node sandbox without DOM stubs
acceptance_assertions.scenario: load rules-engine module in a Node sandbox with no window/document globals
acceptance_assertions.expected_result: module loads and exported functions execute without referencing window, document, or any DOM API

out_of_scope: betting economy beyond a nominal per-hand bet

sections.name: strategy-table
sections.charter: Pure JavaScript module encoding the standard basic-strategy table for the locked S17 + DAS + no-surrender variant: hard totals 5-21, soft totals A2-A9, and pair splits AA-TT, each keyed by player hand category and dealer upcard. Exposes a single recommendation function that returns one action label (Hit/Stand/Double/Split) for any (player hand state, dealer upcard) pair. Single source of truth for both proactive hints and post-hand correctness scoring. No DOM, no rendering, no chart display.
sections.acceptance: For any legal player hand state and dealer upcard, returns the basic-strategy-correct action under the locked ruleset. The table covers hard totals 5-21, soft totals A2-A9, and pair splits AA-TT. The module is data-only plus a lookup function; it does not render or expose chart visuals.

acceptance_assertions.from_acceptance_phrase: returns the basic-strategy-correct action under the locked ruleset
acceptance_assertions.scenario: spot checks across the table: hard 16 vs dealer 10 -> Hit; pair 8s vs dealer 10 -> Split; soft 18 (A,7) vs dealer 9 -> Hit; pair Aces vs anything -> Split; hard 11 vs dealer 5 -> Double; hard 20 vs dealer A -> Stand
acceptance_assertions.expected_result: each lookup returns the expected action label

acceptance_assertions.from_acceptance_phrase: table covers hard totals 5-21, soft totals A2-A9, and pair splits AA-TT
acceptance_assertions.scenario: enumerate every (category, total/pair, dealer upcard 2-A) cell
acceptance_assertions.expected_result: every cell yields a defined action label; no undefined/null returns from recommendation()

acceptance_assertions.from_acceptance_phrase: data-only plus a lookup function; it does not render or expose chart visuals
acceptance_assertions.scenario: module's exported surface inspected
acceptance_assertions.expected_result: no exported function returns HTML, no exported function manipulates DOM, no chart-rendering helper present

out_of_scope: rendering the chart for the user

sections.name: game-state-machine
sections.charter: Pure JavaScript module that orchestrates the hand lifecycle (betting -> dealing -> player decisions -> dealer play -> resolution -> review -> next), holds the canonical game state, exposes a subscribe/dispatch API for UI and hint/review consumers, and records a per-hand decision log capturing each player decision with the player hand snapshot and dealer upcard at decision time. Calls into rules-engine for all rules questions and into strategy-table for the correct-action recording inside each decision-log entry. No DOM.
sections.acceptance: Drives a hand from betting through resolution and into a review state, transitioning only via legal actions per rules-engine. Builds a decision log per hand with one entry per player decision, each entry annotated with the basic-strategy correct action from strategy-table. Exposes a subscribe(listener) API that fires on every state change. Provides a dispatch(action) entry point that accepts user actions and a 'next hand' command that returns the machine to a dealable state without page reload.

acceptance_assertions.from_acceptance_phrase: Drives a hand from betting through resolution and into a review state
acceptance_assertions.scenario: from initial 'betting' phase: dispatch deal -> phase 'player_turn'; dispatch legal action(s) until stand or bust -> phase 'dealer_turn' -> phase 'resolved' -> phase 'review' (or resolved with review payload available)
acceptance_assertions.expected_result: phase transitions occur in the listed order; no illegal transitions accepted

acceptance_assertions.from_acceptance_phrase: transitioning only via legal actions per rules-engine
acceptance_assertions.scenario: dispatch an illegal action (e.g., 'split' when hand is not a pair, or 'double' after a hit)
acceptance_assertions.expected_result: state machine rejects the dispatch (no state mutation, error or noop) and does not advance the phase

acceptance_assertions.from_acceptance_phrase: Builds a decision log per hand with one entry per player decision, each entry annotated with the basic-strategy correct action from strategy-table
acceptance_assertions.scenario: play a hand with 3 player decisions; read getDecisionLog()
acceptance_assertions.expected_result: log has length 3; each entry contains { playerHandSnapshot, dealerUpcard, chosenAction, correctAction } and correctAction matches strategy-table.recommendation(playerHandSnapshot, dealerUpcard)

acceptance_assertions.from_acceptance_phrase: Exposes a subscribe(listener) API that fires on every state change
acceptance_assertions.scenario: register a listener, then dispatch deal then hit; count listener invocations
acceptance_assertions.expected_result: listener fires at least once for each phase or state mutation; receives the latest state snapshot

acceptance_assertions.from_acceptance_phrase: a 'next hand' command that returns the machine to a dealable state without page reload
acceptance_assertions.scenario: from 'resolved'/'review' phase, dispatch the 'next' (or 'deal') command
acceptance_assertions.expected_result: phase returns to 'betting' or directly 'player_turn' on a freshly dealt hand; no page reload required; state.handsPlayed (or equivalent) increments

out_of_scope: displaying hint or review (state machine only emits the data)

sections.name: ui-render
sections.charter: DOM-rendering layer that subscribes to the game-state-machine and paints the table area, dealer hand, player hand(s) including any split sub-hands, action buttons (Hit/Stand/Double/Split), the Hint button, and the Deal/Next button. Buttons are enabled/disabled per phase and per the legal-actions set in current state. Renders the post-hand review panel structure (the hint-and-review section populates the recommendation data). Pure render: receives state, emits user-action callbacks via dispatch.
sections.acceptance: The page renders the dealer hand, player hand(s), action buttons, Hint button, Deal/Next button, and a post-hand review panel container. Action buttons are enabled only when the corresponding action is legal in the current phase. The Deal/Next button is live (enabled) in both 'betting' and 'resolved' phases so the user can deal a new hand without page reload. The Hint button is live during the player's turn. Render updates respond to every state-machine notification.

acceptance_assertions.from_acceptance_phrase: renders the dealer hand, player hand(s), action buttons, Hint button, Deal/Next button, and a post-hand review panel container
acceptance_assertions.scenario: DOM after init
acceptance_assertions.expected_result: DOM contains identifiable elements for: dealer hand region, player hand region (capable of multiple sub-hands), action buttons hit/stand/double/split, hint button, deal/next button, review panel container

acceptance_assertions.from_acceptance_phrase: Action buttons are enabled only when the corresponding action is legal in the current phase
acceptance_assertions.scenario: during phase 'player_turn' on an initial 2-card hand, then after one hit
acceptance_assertions.expected_result: double and split buttons are enabled on the initial 2-card hand if rules-engine reports them legal; after a hit, double and split become disabled; hit/stand remain enabled

acceptance_assertions.from_acceptance_phrase: The Deal/Next button is live (enabled) in both 'betting' and 'resolved' phases so the user can deal a new hand without page reload
acceptance_assertions.scenario: play a hand to resolution; without reloading the page, click the Deal/Next button
acceptance_assertions.expected_result: button is enabled in 'resolved' phase; clicking it returns the app to a dealable state; a new hand is dealt; this works without any page reload

acceptance_assertions.from_acceptance_phrase: The Hint button is live during the player's turn
acceptance_assertions.scenario: during 'player_turn' phase, before any action is taken
acceptance_assertions.expected_result: Hint button is enabled and clickable

acceptance_assertions.from_acceptance_phrase: Render updates respond to every state-machine notification
acceptance_assertions.scenario: subscribe a spy listener AND check DOM after each dispatch (deal, hit, stand)
acceptance_assertions.expected_result: DOM reflects the new state on every notification (cards rendered, button states updated)

out_of_scope: rules logic (consume rules-engine indirectly via state machine)
page boilerplate / global styles (lives in app-shell)

sections.name: hint-and-review
sections.charter: Wires the Hint button and the post-hand review panel content. On hint click, queries strategy-table for the recommended action given the current player hand and dealer upcard, and surfaces a single action label into the hint area. After every hand resolves, walks the state-machine's decision log and renders, into the review panel, one row per decision showing the player's chosen action, the basic-strategy correct action, and a match indicator. Consumes ui-render's review panel container; does not own page layout.
sections.acceptance: Clicking the Hint button mid-hand reveals the basic-strategy action for the current state, sourced from strategy-table. After every resolved hand, the post-hand review panel appears and lists each decision the user made vs basic-strategy correct, with one row per decision. The hint shows a single action label only (no reasoning text, no chart visualization).

acceptance_assertions.from_acceptance_phrase: Clicking the Hint button mid-hand reveals the basic-strategy action for the current state
acceptance_assertions.scenario: during 'player_turn' phase with a known hand state (e.g., player 16 vs dealer 10), click the Hint button
acceptance_assertions.expected_result: the hint area shows the action label that strategy-table.recommendation returns for that state (e.g., 'Hit' for 16v10)

acceptance_assertions.from_acceptance_phrase: After every resolved hand, the post-hand review panel appears and lists each decision the user made vs basic-strategy correct, with one row per decision
acceptance_assertions.scenario: play a full hand involving N>=2 player decisions, then let it resolve
acceptance_assertions.expected_result: review panel becomes visible; renders exactly N rows; each row shows chosenAction, correctAction, and a match indicator; for any deliberately suboptimal play the row is marked as a mismatch

acceptance_assertions.from_acceptance_phrase: The hint shows a single action label only (no reasoning text, no chart visualization)
acceptance_assertions.scenario: hint area inspected while a hint is being shown
acceptance_assertions.expected_result: no explanatory paragraph, no chart/cell highlight, no , no ; only the action label text

out_of_scope: the strategy data itself (lives in strategy-table)
decision logging (state machine owns the log)
DOM layout of the review container (ui-render owns the container)

sections.name: app-shell
sections.charter: Top-level page wiring: index.html, styles.css, and the small bootstrap script that constructs the rules-engine, strategy-table, game-state-machine, ui-render, and hint-and-review modules and wires them together (state machine <-> ui-render subscription, ui-render dispatch -> state machine, hint-and-review consumes both). Provides the page chrome (table felt area, header) and desktop-first responsive layout. Contains no rules logic, no strategy data, no decision logic.
sections.acceptance: Loading index.html in a modern browser produces a working blackjack trainer single-page app with no build step required: dealer hand, player hand(s), action buttons, Hint button, Deal/Next button, and a review panel area are all present, and the modules are correctly wired so dispatching a player action flows through the state machine and re-renders the UI. Layout is desktop-first responsive (usable on mobile but not optimized).

acceptance_assertions.from_acceptance_phrase: Loading index.html in a modern browser produces a working blackjack trainer single-page app with no build step required
acceptance_assertions.scenario: open output/integration/index.html directly (no bundler/build) in a modern browser
acceptance_assertions.expected_result: app boots, no missing-module errors in console, no 404s, all script tags resolve relative paths that exist in the integrated artifact

acceptance_assertions.from_acceptance_phrase: dealer hand, player hand(s), action buttons, Hint button, Deal/Next button, and a review panel area are all present
acceptance_assertions.scenario: DOM after page load
acceptance_assertions.expected_result: all six surfaces are present in the rendered DOM

acceptance_assertions.from_acceptance_phrase: the modules are correctly wired so dispatching a player action flows through the state machine and re-renders the UI
acceptance_assertions.scenario: click Deal, then click Hit, then click Stand
acceptance_assertions.expected_result: each click advances state and updates the rendered DOM (cards appear, button states change, eventually a resolved review appears)

acceptance_assertions.from_acceptance_phrase: Layout is desktop-first responsive (usable on mobile but not optimized)
acceptance_assertions.scenario: inspect styles.css for at least one viewport-width media query and a flexible/fluid layout primitive (flex/grid/% widths)
acceptance_assertions.expected_result: stylesheet contains responsive scaffolding; layout does not break (no horizontal scroll, no overlapping critical controls) at common mobile widths

out_of_scope: persistence / localStorage (not required by the core experience)

sections.name: edge-case-testing
sections.charter: Cover every acceptance assertion with `verifier: edge_case_testing` from the section list. Every section's `acceptance_assertions[]` is in scope. Build a runnable test harness that exercises the integrated artifact (DOM + wired modules) and verifies each `edge_case_testing` assertion across sections 1-6 and each `behavior` / `presence` / `absence` / `user_flow` IP-level assertion that is testable against the integrated artifact. Tests must drive real user-flow sequences (deal -> play -> resolve -> next-hand) end-to-end, not just unit-level shims, so that drift like 'Deal button disabled in resolved phase' is caught.
sections.acceptance: A runnable test harness exists in the integrated artifact and reports pass/fail per assertion id (S1.A1 through S6.A3, plus the IP-level assertions). Every acceptance assertion tagged `verifier: edge_case_testing` in sections-v1.json is covered by at least one test case. The harness simulates real user flows: after a hand resolves, the harness clicks the Deal/Next button and verifies a new hand starts without page reload; the harness clicks Hint mid-hand and verifies the action label appears; the harness completes a hand and verifies the review panel renders one row per decision.

acceptance_assertions.from_acceptance_phrase: A runnable test harness exists in the integrated artifact and reports pass/fail per assertion id
acceptance_assertions.scenario: test harness runs (in Node + jsdom or in-browser) and emits a structured pass/fail report
acceptance_assertions.expected_result: report enumerates every assertion id covered, with verdict per id

acceptance_assertions.from_acceptance_phrase: Every acceptance assertion tagged `verifier: edge_case_testing` in sections-v1.json is covered by at least one test case
acceptance_assertions.scenario: cross-reference sections-v1.json acceptance_assertions where verifier == 'edge_case_testing' against test harness coverage
acceptance_assertions.expected_result: 100% coverage; no edge_case_testing-tagged assertion missing a test

acceptance_assertions.from_acceptance_phrase: after a hand resolves, the harness clicks the Deal/Next button and verifies a new hand starts without page reload
acceptance_assertions.scenario: harness plays a hand to resolution; harness clicks Deal/Next; harness asserts no page-reload occurred and a new hand is in 'player_turn' or 'betting' phase
acceptance_assertions.expected_result: test passes; documented as covering S3.A5 and S4.A3

acceptance_assertions.from_acceptance_phrase: the harness clicks Hint mid-hand and verifies the action label appears
acceptance_assertions.scenario: harness deals a hand, clicks Hint during 'player_turn'
acceptance_assertions.expected_result: hint surface contains a single action label from the set {Hit,Stand,Double,Split}; documented as covering S5.A1

acceptance_assertions.from_acceptance_phrase: the harness completes a hand and verifies the review panel renders one row per decision
acceptance_assertions.scenario: harness plays a hand with N>=2 decisions, lets it resolve
acceptance_assertions.expected_result: review panel renders exactly N rows, each with chosenAction, correctAction, and match indicator; documented as covering S5.A2

out_of_scope: writing or amending production code (tests only)
verifying assertions tagged `cv_artifact_exercise` or `critic_final_sweep` (different verifier owns those)
